subject: Re: CFT fitting range setting for batch pre-processing in Warp
body:
Hi Cyan,

I've had good results without grouping tilt series, and the varying defocus
was handled correctly (assuming that this is a single-collection dataset;
if your defoci differ a lot, I guess splitting the dataset is a better
idea).
If you're having troubles with CTF not fitting well, make sure you skip the
first CTF peak (even for the lesser defocused tilt series); I've been
bitten by this several times...
[image: image.png]

Good luck!

Cheers,
Lorenzo

On Wed, Feb 23, 2022 at 4:21 PM Cyan Ching <xingyichengcyanching@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Dear Warp/M users,
>
> Greetings!
>
> How do you set a single CTF fitting range for batch pre-processing in
> Warp? Do you group tomograms based on their defocus? Any tips you'd like to
> share?
>
> Thank you!
>
> Best regards,
>
> Cyan Ching
>
> PhD student
>
> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>
